file_insights.scope: InstallHooks command variant and platform-specific handlers
modifications: Added `uninstall: bool` field to InstallHooks enum variant with clap attribute
Refactored install_hooks_macos() signature to accept uninstall parameter
Refactored install_hooks_linux() signature to accept uninstall parameter
Implemented macOS uninstall logic: launchctl unload + fs::remove_file
Added conditional uninstall handling for Linux (stub only)
Updated main command dispatcher to extract and pass uninstall flag

patterns_discovered.name: Bidirectional Service Management
patterns_discovered.description: Added inverse operation to install_hooks by making uninstall a flag parameter rather than separate command variant
patterns_discovered.benefit: Maintains cohesion between install and uninstall paths in single function

patterns_discovered.name: Conditional Compilation for Platform Abstraction
patterns_discovered.description: Leveraging #[cfg(target_os = "..")] to provide OS-specific implementations with graceful stubs
patterns_discovered.benefit: Code compiles on all platforms but provides proper implementations only where applicable

patterns_discovered.name: Graceful Service Lifecycle Management
patterns_discovered.description: Using launchctl with output capture to detect and report unload failures without hard-failing
patterns_discovered.benefit: Handles case where service is not loaded without breaking uninstall flow

patterns_discovered.name: Path Resolution Hygiene
patterns_discovered.description: Moved HOME environment variable resolution earlier in function to support both install and uninstall paths
patterns_discovered.benefit: DRY principle: HOME resolved once at function start rather than duplicated

gotchas_discovered.description: Linux uninstall logic remains unimplemented with only eprintln stub
gotchas_discovered.implication: Users on Linux cannot uninstall merge-gate hooks; would require systemctl disable + stop

gotchas_discovered.issue: No idempotency safeguard for macOS uninstall
gotchas_discovered.description: Calling uninstall twice attempts launchctl unload on non-existent plist; handled gracefully but could warn earlier
gotchas_discovered.implication: Second uninstall will show 'no hook installed' message rather than success

gotchas_discovered.issue: No confirmation prompt for destructive operation
gotchas_discovered.description: Uninstall removes plist without requiring user confirmation
gotchas_discovered.implication: Accidental invocation of --uninstall will immediately remove service configuration

approach_outcome.summary: Implemented --uninstall flag for InstallHooks command with full macOS support and Linux stub
approach_outcome.completeness: Partial - macOS fully implemented, Linux deferred
quality_signals: Symmetrical API design (uninstall as flag on install command)
User-friendly console output with visual feedback
Handles edge case of non-loaded service gracefully

recommendations.details: Replace eprintln stub with: systemctl --user disable sh.bop.merge-gate && systemctl --user stop sh.bop.merge-gate, handling ENOENT gracefully

recommendations.action: Add --force flag for uninstall without launchctl unload step
recommendations.details: Allow direct plist removal if launchctl fails or is unavailable

recommendations.action: Add integration tests for install/uninstall round-trip
recommendations.details: Verify plist created on install, removed on uninstall, and idempotent behavior

recommendations.action: Document uninstall behavior in help text
recommendations.details: Clarify that --uninstall removes service configuration files
